content.title: Department Architecture
content.subtitle: Architecting a Web Development Department from Zero
content.description: Engineered a complete departmental infrastructure from the ground up. By establishing automated CI/CD pipelines, standardizing project boilerplates, and enforcing strict quality KPIs, I transformed an unstructured environment into a high-velocity production engine for a team of 6 developers in under a year.

content.title: Proprietary Middleware
content.subtitle: Custom Enterprise Middleware API
content.description: When the legacy base API lacked the rich features required for complex client builds, I architected a proprietary solution. This custom middleware bridges the data gap, serving as the mandatory, high-performance routing foundation that now powers 100% of our agency's client websites.

content.title: Anime Helper
content.subtitle: Declarative Animation Micro-Framework
content.description: Abstracted the powerful anime.js library into a custom, declarative micro-framework. This proprietary engine eliminates boilerplate by managing complex lifecycles, global preset registration, and accessibility compliance (reduced motion) under the hood, allowing developers to trigger complex, staggered scroll-animations with a single configuration object.

content.title: The Deterministic Structure of Local Prime Distribution
content.subtitle: A Unified Computational and Geometric Framework
content.description:
The distribution of prime numbers has traditionally been modeled using probabilistic methods, such as the Prime Number Theorem, which describes the asymptotic density of primes but does not predict their exact locations. This paper introduces a novel computational and geometric framework for understanding local prime distribution as a deterministic phenomenon, governed by modular constraints and fractal hierarchy.

Unlike traditional algebraic sieves that filter numbers based on divisibility rules (e.g., the Sieve of Eratosthenes), the "Path of Least Resistance" (PLR) framework operationalizes modular constraints to filter by structural geometry. We prove that the location of the next prime is not a probabilistic event but a deterministic outcome: it is always the next geometrically-valid candidate with the minimum gap from the current prime.

Furthermore, we prove that this sieve is Fractal. As the Primorial resolution increases, the structural resistance of the "Clean Channel", the set of valid candidates between primes, decreases systematically toward zero (0.00008). This convergence creates a geometric condition where primes flow through positions of least resistance, suggesting a deterministic structure underlying the distribution of primes.

content.title: THE RECURSIVE GEOMETRY OF PRIME PREDICTION
content.subtitle: DERIVING A DETERMINISTIC TRAJECTORY VIA STRUCTURAL SELF-EXCLUSION
content.description: The distribution of prime numbers is historically modeled as a probabilistic phenomenon, famously described by the Prime Number Theorem and the Riemann Hypothesis. While sieve methods effectively identify primes by removing composites, they are traditionally viewed as subtractive search algorithms rather than predictive functions. This paper introduces the Recursive Geometric Sieve, a constructive framework that transforms prime discovery into a deterministic trajectory calculation. We identify the "Structure of the Imposter", composite numbers that mimic local prime geometry, as the interference pattern of previous primorial waves. To resolve boundary failures in recursive sieving, we introduce the Self-Factor Exclusion Rule, a logical operator that permits prime waves to exist at their own harmonic frequencies. Benchmarking confirms that this model achieves 100.00% predictive accuracy over 100,000,000 iterations when the structural resolution scales with N. Furthermore, we propose that this recursive mechanism offers a geometric corollary to the Riemann Hypothesis, framing the distribution of primes as the interference product of deterministic modular waves.
